assistant_personality.role: senior infrastructure-as-code architect / senior platform architect
output_rules: run tools first, show results, then stop
no preamble, no pleasantries, no narration
deliver zipped artifacts when project phase completes
actively identify weak assumptions and edge cases without being asked

user_preferences.deliverables: production-ready, fully documented, zipped on completion

project.name: vault-terraform
project.goal: Fully declarative Vault configuration — KV v2 engines, HCL policies, AppRole auth, credential storage, sample secrets — deployed via Terraform and GitOps
project.status: COMPLETE — all resources defined, fully documented inline, zipped and delivered

services.name: network-atm-secrets
services.purpose: ATM network device credentials + all AppRole bootstrap credential storage

services.name: auth/approle
services.purpose: AppRole auth for AWX and SMS consumers — 8 roles total

databases.name: Terraform remote state
databases.notes: Template provided in environments/dev/backend.hcl — not yet populated with real values

secret_flows.description: Terraform generates secret_ids → writes role_id+secret_id to network-atm-secrets KV → consuming system reads from KV → authenticates to AppRole → receives scoped token
Application token (nwpci-self-secret-id policy) → POST /v1/auth/approle/role/<role>/secret-id → store new secret_id → revoke old via accessor

integrations.access: read-only (ro policies) on respective KV engines
full CRUD (rw policies) on respective KV engines

integrations.auth: CI AppRole (provisioned out-of-band) + OIDC to AWS for S3 state

decisions.decision: All AppRole bootstrap credentials stored in network-atm-secrets regardless of domain
decisions.rationale: Centralises credential management; avoids cross-engine policy complexity

decisions.decision: Policy HCL generated via .tftpl templates parameterised by mount path
decisions.rationale: Eliminates copy-paste drift; guarantees capability consistency across all four engines

decisions.decision: AppRole auth backend enabled once in root/auth.tf; modules use data source reference
decisions.rationale: Prevents resource conflict when the approle module is instantiated eight times
One shared rotation policy for all roles; avoids eight duplicate policies

decisions.decision: Sample secrets written as for_each resource in root/main.tf, not via kv_secret module
decisions.rationale: Sample payload schema (username/password) differs from credential schema (role_id/secret_id)
Suits long-running daemons; operators override to 1 for single-use CI jobs per environment
Preserves audit trail via soft-delete rather than hard-purge on terraform destroy

decisions.decision: environment variable validated at plan time against allowlist [dev, staging, prod]
decisions.rationale: Prevents KV path injection from typos; fails fast at plan not apply

decisions.decision: All secret_id outputs marked sensitive=true throughout the module chain
decisions.rationale: Redacted in plan/apply console; accessible only via terraform output -json with deliberate intent

decisions.decision: Makefile destroy target requires typed environment name confirmation
decisions.rationale: Prevents accidental terraform destroy in staging or production

constraints: No hardcoded secrets in any .tf file
terraform.tfvars must never be committed to source control (.gitignored)
*.tfstate must never be committed to source control
Terraform provider pinned at ~> 4.4.0; no alpha/beta features
CI apply only on push to main or explicit workflow_dispatch — no auto-apply on PR
PCI-DSS naming convention enforced: nwpci- prefix on all roles and policies
Credential expiry metadata required on every secret (expiry_date, pwd_rotation_data, pwd_rotation_method)
S3 remote state must have SSE encryption enabled in all non-local environments

risks.risk: nwpci-self-secret-id wildcard policy allows any AppRole token to generate a secret_id for any role
risks.mitigation: Acceptable with current trust model; replace + with literal role name if zero-trust between roles is required

risks.risk: Bootstrap secret_ids stored in Terraform state — plaintext on disk if S3 backend not configured
risks.mitigation: Enforce S3 backend with SSE before any team-shared or CI apply

risks.risk: secret_id_ttl = 0 means a never-rotated secret_id is valid indefinitely
risks.mitigation: Set finite secret_id_ttl (e.g. 720h) in staging/prod; ensure rotation tooling is operational

risks.risk: AWX credential path contains literal 'dev' prefix regardless of target environment
risks.mitigation: Confirm path convention with operations team; may need to change to awx/ prefix

risks.risk: CI AppRole is not managed by this Terraform config — must be provisioned out-of-band
risks.mitigation: Document bootstrap procedure; create separate Terraform workspace for CI AppRole provisioning

risks.question: Does SMS rotation tooling consume pwd_rotation_data / expiry_date metadata, or does a separate scheduler drive rotation?
Vault seal type (Shamir vs cloud KMS) — determines whether seal_wrap should be enabled on KV engines

risks.description: credential_expiry_date is a static string shared across all credentials — real use requires per-role or per-secret expiry driven by a rotation management layer
No Vault audit log backend configured — required for PCI compliance; vault_audit resource missing from current config

coding_conventions.module_structure: each module has main.tf / variables.tf / outputs.tf — no merged files
coding_conventions.resource_naming: singleton resources within a module use 'this' as the resource name
coding_conventions.documentation: every resource attribute has an explanatory inline comment; every variable uses heredoc description block
coding_conventions.policy_generation: template-driven via .tftpl files — never inline heredoc for repeated patterns
coding_conventions.iteration: for_each over count for stable key-addressed resource sets
coding_conventions.dependencies: explicit depends_on where Terraform cannot infer from references
coding_conventions.sensitive_outputs: marked sensitive=true at every layer in the module output chain

next_actions.action: Add vault_audit resource to root config
next_actions.detail: File or syslog backend required for PCI compliance — current config has no audit log backend
Create separate Terraform workspace or manual bootstrap for CI service account AppRole with minimal policy scope

next_actions.action: Populate environments/dev/backend.hcl with real S3 values
next_actions.detail: Set bucket, key, region, dynamodb_table, role_arn; enable SSE; test with make init ENV=dev
Add backend.hcl and terraform.tfvars per environment; set secret_id_num_uses=1 and finite secret_id_ttl in prod
Restrict AppRole secret_id usage to known AWX and SMS host IP ranges
Clarify whether global/vault/dev/<env>/<role> is correct for staging/prod or should use global/vault/awx/<env>/<role>

next_actions.action: Document and wire rotation tooling integration
next_actions.detail: Confirm which system reads expiry_date and pwd_rotation_data; determine if Terraform needs to manage rotation schedule or only metadata schema

restore_prompt: You are a senior infrastructure-as-code architect and senior platform engineer. Resume this session using all architecture state, decisions, constraints, and risks in this document as authoritative. Do not revisit settled design decisions. Do not add preamble, pleasantries, or narration. Run tools first, show results, then stop. Active project: vault-terraform — a Terraform configuration managing HashiCorp Vault KV v2 engines, AppRole auth, HCL policies, credential storage, and sample secrets for a Network PCI platform. The codebase is complete and zipped. Continue from next_actions in priority order. Coding standards: full inline documentation on all files, implementation-first, production-ready, tools-first output style, no filler.
